---
title: '#Religiholics - Recovering from addiction to religion'
date: '2014-09-15T15:16:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '7791132553676347588'
blogger_filename: '/2014/09/religiholics-recovering-from-addiction.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Spiritual Growth'
---

<br /><br /><br /><table align="center" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="tr-caption-container" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; text-align: center;"><tbody><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/stainglass-924d26425137.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"><img alt="Stainglass Church Window, Attributre Wikipedia" border="0" closure_lm_543063="null" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/stainglass-924d26425137.jpg" dua="true" height="320" title="Stainglass Church Window, Attributre Wikipedia" width="231" /></a></td></tr><tr><td class="tr-caption" style="text-align: center;">Stainglass Church Window, <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stained_glass" target="_blank">Attributre Wikipedia</a></td></tr></tbody></table><br /><h3>Our job is to leave religion behind, and move with God's next move. </h3><br />In light of all the talk about Islam and 9-11 this week. I've been thinking about religion broadly, and Christian religion more specifically. <br /><h4>&nbsp;</h4><h4>What is a religiholic?</h4><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq">A religiholic: is a person so addicted to religious tradition, that they miss God in the process. </blockquote><br /><h4>What IS religion? </h4><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq">Religion is a set of traditions and doctrines; whereby, man serves his idea of God. </blockquote><br /><br />God's plan is to have relationship, as Father to Son/Daughter. He is constantly moving us to deeper relationship with Him. Each time a mass movement of God starts, it is designed to break us from outward process, and take us to inward progress. <br /><br /><h4>What&nbsp;is Christian Religion?</h4><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq">Christian Religion is the by-product (traditions/doctrines), left over, when God's move turns from movement to monument. &nbsp;</blockquote>Thereby, man turns God's movement away from the traditions and doctrines, back into new traditions and doctrines. The crust hardens, the life is drained, and the denomination is born. God begins a new movement, and it starts again. <br /><br /><br />---<br /><h4>And now I leave you with a question:</h4><blockquote class="tr_bq"><b></b><br /><h4><b>Are you serving God... or your idea of Him? </b></h4><b></b></blockquote><span style="font-family: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif;">To Respond Leave A Comment on This Post</span><br /><br /><h4><div><script src="https://apis.google.com/js/plusone.js"></script><g:plus action="share"></g:plus></div></h4><h4>By&nbsp;<a href="https://plus.google.com/+DarrellWolfe/posts" target="_blank">Darrell Wolfe</a></h4><div><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/about-20.html" style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif;" target="_blank">About This Blog, and Darrell.&nbsp;</a><br /><br /></div><div><h4><br /><span style="font-weight: bold;">This Weeks Resource:</span></h4><div><a href="http://teddekker.com/2008/11/04/what-order-to-read-teds-books/">Ted Dekker: Circle Series</a>... that should help break some of the religious crust off. <br /><span style="font-family: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif;"><i><span style="line-height: 16px;">Affiliate Disclosure:</span></i></span><br /><span style="font-family: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif;"><i><span style="line-height: 16px;">I earn&nbsp;commissions&nbsp;off of some of my suggestions.&nbsp;</span></i><i style="line-height: 16px;">Full Disclaimers (<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/about-20.html" target="_blank">Click Here</a>)</i><br /><i>See my Store for more suggestions (<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/books-resources.html" rel="nofollow" target="_blank">Click Here</a>)</i></span></div><div>- <br /><br /><!-- Begin MailChimp Signup Form --><link href="//cdn-images.mailchimp.com/embedcode/slim-081711.css" rel="stylesheet" type="text/css"></link><style type="text/css"> #mc_embed_signup{background:#fff; clear:left; font:14px Helvetica,Arial,sans-serif;  width:200px;}  /* Add your own MailChimp form style overrides in your site stylesheet or in this style block.     We recommend moving this block and the preceding CSS link to the HEAD of your HTML file. */ </style><br /><div id="mc_embed_signup"><form action="//darrellwolfe.us3.list-manage.com/subscribe/post?u=979cb0db6ca74343e82a059ba&amp;id=14df3ba97b" class="validate" id="mc-embedded-subscribe-form" method="post" name="mc-embedded-subscribe-form" novalidate="" target="_blank"><label for="mce-EMAIL">Subscribe By Email</label> <input class="email" id="mce-EMAIL" name="EMAIL" placeholder="email address" required="" type="email" /> <!-- real people should not fill this in and expect good things - do not remove this or risk form bot signups--><br /><div style="left: -5000px; position: absolute;"><input name="b_979cb0db6ca74343e82a059ba_14df3ba97b" tabindex="-1" /></div><div class="clear"><input class="button" id="mc-embedded-subscribe" name="subscribe" type="submit" value="Subscribe" /></div></form></div><!--End mc_embed_signup--><br /><br /><br /></div></div>
